mnemonics: Remember the acronym 'TP-FN-FP-TN' by thinking: 'True Positives are Fantastic, False Negatives are Frustrating, False Positives are Frightening, True Negatives are Terrific'.
keyPoints: A confusion matrix is a table used to evaluate the performance of a classification model by comparing actual vs predicted values.
The four main outcomes are: True Positives (TP), True Negatives (TN), False Positives (FP), and False Negatives (FN).
Key metrics like Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score are derived from the values in the confusion matrix.
commonMistakes: Confusing False Positives (Type I error) with False Negatives (Type II error).
Assuming a high accuracy score always means a good model, which can be misleading if the dataset is imbalanced.
